failure_modes.text: Jumping into analysis too early can lock the team into tools before the underlying structure of the problem is clear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Validate the problem frame before choosing an analysis tool: spend time defining the problem structure so the tool fits the cause, not the other way around.

failure_modes.text: Symptom chasing leaves the deeper machine-level driver untouched, so the problem recurs.
failure_modes.mitigation: Go deeper than the first plausible explanation: require at least three 'why' iterations before accepting a root cause, to avoid fixing symptoms while the driver persists.

failure_modes.text: RCA breaks when the search for one neat root cause erases a causal stack of interacting failures.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the search for one root cause erases a causal stack
failure_modes.mitigation: Allow for multiple interacting causes: when a single root cause feels too neat, map the causal stack and check whether removing just one factor would actually prevent recurrence.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Is the outcome good or bad, and if bad, is the design bad or is the responsible party incapable?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the outcome good or bad? Who is responsible for the outcome? If the outcome is bad, is the Responsible Party incapable and/or is the design bad?

heuristics.text: Use the Five Whys to push past the surface explanation toward the real generative driver.
Disaggregate the problem with logic trees so the full causal picture is visible before action.
Use fishbone diagrams to trace causes of causes instead of stopping at the first plausible mechanism.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on anti-symptom diagnosis, causal probing, and structured disaggregation.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor process sprawl because the raw source is stronger on Five Whys, logic trees, and anti-premature-analysis discipline.
open_questions: The third premortem item condenses the Dalio-loop diagnostic questions into a chunk that stays reusable across contexts.

allies: The raw ally section is strong on systems-thinking, feynman-technique, and critical-thinking as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist section is strong on confirmation-bias and law-of-the-instrument, but only the cleanest canonical ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source is tension-real around symptom chasing, correlation errors, and anti-confirmation discipline even without a dedicated structured tension block.
